body:
I noticed a regression:

1. Create a sticky note and write line long enough to wrap one the next line. Confirm.
2. Edit again and delete the text, then write a single-line text. Confirm.
3. The text is misaligned.

	![excal-regr-sticky](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/148832258-f76f7a9f-656f-42a7-aa8e-ff3029848b85.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
> I noticed a regression:
> 
> 1. Create a sticky note and write line long enough to wrap one the next line. Confirm.
> 2. Edit again and delete the text, then write a single-line text. Confirm.
> 3. The text is misaligned.
>    ![excal-regr-sticky](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/148832258-f76f7a9f-656f-42a7-aa8e-ff3029848b85.gif)

Good catch!. Updated the implementation to cache `originalText` and comparing with editor value instead. Added spec for this case. Should be fine now.

body: I've also added  `window` `focus` event. This will catch cases of having both tabs visible or partially visible.
author: dwelle

body:
The failing tests were caused because blurring the wysiwyg was for some reason firing `change` event on `window`, which in turn overwritten the elements with empty `[]` because in tests we don't persist state to LS.

But this makes me wonder — the `change` event we're now listening for will be triggered in many cases like picking a file from the filesystem, thus potentially overwriting the state with stale data in case the LS isn't updated in time. This could happen, since dialogs are generally thread blocking. Also could happen once we move to IDB which is async.

Also note that even `visibilitychange` alone could be affected by this.
author: dwelle

body: Another, related thing: we need to disable the syncing when collaborating, because we're not syncing to LS during collab.
author: dwelle

body:
> I've also added `window` `focus` event. This will catch cases of having both tabs visible or partially visible.

Why do we need `focus` event ? coz this will trigger LS sync when ever window gains focus even when visible eg switching focus from devtools to Excalidraw window, also the `visibilityChange` will be triggered when ever the page becomes fully visible from partial visible/minimised state as well.
author: ad1992

body:
> Why do we need `focus` event ? coz this will trigger LS sync when ever window gains focus even when visible eg switching focus from devtools to Excalidraw window, also the `visibilityChange` will be triggered when ever the page becomes fully visible from partial visible/minimised state as well.

`visibilityChange` will give us only a subset of `focus` (except for one special case). We want to handle cases like when you have both tabs visible (on one or two monitors) and switch between them.

Either way, we want to make the syncing robust enough so we don't have to think whether `change` event can cause race conditions. It must work properly no matter what.

body:
I checked, and realized this is kinda a hot path, not just for excalidraw.com, but for host apps as well. For example, we call `updateScene` in collaboration on every update, and pass in the whole scene elements. Restoring them unnecessarily can quickly become a bottleneck (excalidraw collaboration syncing is already super heavy and congests easily as it is).

1. We can make this optional, but I'd rather avoid this — it wouldn't really solve the issue. 
2. Second option is to optimize the restoring calls.

	This would likely consist of versioning of some kind so we can bail if the version is up to date.
3. Third option is to call restore manually before passing data to `updateScene`, in places where the data comes from external boundaries where we're not reasonably certain they are already restored.

@zsviczian @ad1992
author: dwelle

body: I think best would be to come up with (2) to optimise restore calls, (3) is upto the host if they want to restore they always can and we can recommend it in readme as well to make sure migrations work properly
author: ad1992

body:
> I think best would be to come up with (2) to optimise restore calls, (3) is upto the host if they want to restore they always can and we can recommend it in readme as well to make sure migrations work properly

Well, if we wanna put the responsibility onto hosts, then there's not (that much) need for (2).

body:
> This is very nice. I would recommend adding the link property to all elements, not just text elements. What will be the trigger for the hyperlink dialog to appear? or will it always pop up above the object when the object is selected? How will hyperlinks operate in View mode?

The state it's in now doesn't represent the latest design, which we'll push here soon.

In short, what we're going for right now is this:

1. by default, the popup will not show for any element until you create the link ↓
2. you create the link by clicking a link icon from the sidebar menu, which will open the popup over the element
3. once the initial popup is confirmed and the link created, the popup will then show when you select the element (on `pointerup`, for reasons below)
4. when the popup is shown and you start moving/resizing/etc. the element, the popup is hidden to not distract/hinder the editing action (this is why we initially show the popup on `pointerup` instead of `pointerdown` so that there's no popup flicker when you start moving the shape without releasing your pointer button)

All elements will support hyperlinks.

body:
🔥

Here's a UX note: when moving/resizing, after you stop, the popup blocks the view which is distracting or prevents you from visually aligning to other elements, and or prevents you from selecting those occluded elements.

I suggest we don't show the popup if you've previously moved/resized the element (maybe other cases too), we shouldn't show the popup until you click the element again. (though there should be some threshold like 5px because you often make micro-movements when clicking elements).

I think that when you interact with the hyperlinked element in this way, 99% of the time you will not want to edit the hyperlink — and if you do, you can click on the element again to show the popup.

—

Alternatively, if we think the popup is too distracting in general, we could theoretically make it only show on clicking the external-link icon (therefore make the icon not redirect to the site in edit mode).

Hard to say, but we should take Excalidraw's editing experience philosophy into account here and think on this.

![excal-hyperlinks-move-annoying](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/150388138-e8ff2ac7-1050-4b01-b94f-749c43257ed7.gif)

body:
A few notes on the link-icon: 

1. I think we'll want to put a timeout on the tooltip hover as well as there's the same UX issue as with the hyperlink popup: it becomes distracting when moving mouse over the canvas elements that are hyperlinked. Especially since the case for needing to constantly review where the link goes to is even smaller than for showing the popup.
2. We should only open the url if the `pointerup` event is in the same area (size of the icon) as `pointerdown`. This will fix (among other) a case where you happen to start dragging while in the hitbox area of the link icon.
3. The hitbox seems too large. Right now it's around the size of the red rectangle below, which ends up in false positives when you want to select the element (or neighboring elements):

	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152020516-f34eac9b-78d3-44cc-9598-4fc70e3c5bed.png)

4. I don't think the link-icon should be on top of elements with higher z-index:

	(applies to interactivity as well)

	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152021340-0863e24c-9d00-4b36-a72b-ed5f24ee8c8c.png)

author: vercel

body:
@ad1992, this simplifies and resolves the errors (and heuristic "magic") with text positioning in textwysiwyg. This change greatly improves the user experience for Excalidraw when it is rendered embedded in an app with offsetLeft & offsetTop !=0.

Please take a look and let's push this important improvement to production.
author: zsviczian

body: This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️.  Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.
author: ad1992

body:
> This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️. Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.

Great! A second (and 3rd, 4th...) round of testing is always essential. What do you mean by specs?
author: zsviczian

body:
> > This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️. Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.
> 
> Great! A second (and 3rd, 4th...) round of testing is always essential. What do you mean by specs?

I meant tests for the change just to make sure we don't break in future as this part has been broken often in the past.
author: ad1992

body:
> > > This is a great improvement @zsviczian ❤️. Could you also write specs for this ? Will do one round of testing and then should be good to merge.
> > 
> > 
> > Great! A second (and 3rd, 4th...) round of testing is always essential. What do you mean by specs?
> 
> I meant tests for the change just to make sure we don't break in future as this part has been broken often in the past.

I think this broke frequently in the future because there was a formula error. sceneCoordsToViewportCoords was wrong, and we were trying to resolve its impact by applying complex calculations in textwysiwyg.  That should be resolved for good.

Regarding testing... I don't really know how to test for zoom and offset situations... I haven't yet looked at excalidraw tests, I'll take a look to see if this sort of testing can be automated.  My recommendation would be to deploy a second app with every change, the second should have offsets and righthand side margin.
author: zsviczian

body:

>

Sure 👍🏻 .  The tests would probably be tricky for this but lets give it a try and decide accordingly :).

>My recommendation would be to deploy a second app with every change, the second should have offsets and righthand side margin.

What do you mean by deploying an app with offsets and right handed margin ? We have the same code sandbox example running with package example in port 3001 which can be used for testing
author: ad1992

body:
> What do you mean by deploying an app with offsets and right handed margin ? We have the same code sandbox example running with package example in port 3001 which can be used for testing

To make the question concrete. If I would want to test a PR with screen offsets, how can I do that. For sake of example, the current preview release for the hyperlinks PR is https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app/ I tried https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app:3001/ but that did not bring up anything...
author: zsviczian

body:
> > What do you mean by deploying an app with offsets and right handed margin ? We have the same code sandbox example running with package example in port 3001 which can be used for testing
> 
> To make the question concrete. If I would want to test a PR with screen offsets, how can I do that. For sake of example, the current preview release for the hyperlinks PR is https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app/ I tried https://excalidraw-f13roaq8h-excalidraw.vercel.app:3001/ but that did not bring up anything...

Ohh ok I meant it will work in [`localhost:3001`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README_NEXT.md#development), we can also deploy it as well so its available in preview too
author: ad1992

body:
> Ohh ok I meant it will work in [`localhost:3001`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README_NEXT.md#development), we can also deploy it as well so its available in preview too

That would be great! I was looking around the repository but didn't yet have the patience to figure it out. If you give me some pointers I am also happy to propose a PR with port 3001 being available in preview too.


BTW Have you had time to test this fix? I've deployed this to the Obsidian Excalidraw build a week ago and have not experienced any issues since. I am pretty confident that this is working as expected. Would be good to get this deployed before further functions build on the current erroneous sceneCoordsToViewportCoords() function.
author: zsviczian

body: For sure, let's merge! Thanks!

body: @dwelle, please take a look at this when you have time. I've been using this change on the iPad and it is a game-changer for freedraw.  This is the most straightforward solution I can find to the problem of palm rejection given the cross-platform issues of not being able to use the SPen or Pencil SDKs.
author: zsviczian

body:
Hey @zsviczian —  you've beat us to it!

But, I was thinking we should:

1. extend this all tools, not just freedraw 
2. auto-enable this on `pen` event detection (while still allowing to manually disable — although the exact behavior of what should happen in the future afterwards isn't specced out)
3. reject only `touch` events (allow `mouse` events) — though this is an edge case

Nevertheless, we can leave the above for later as users will benefit from even a partial solution.

But, I think we should put the button elsewhere. While it may make sense to put lock buttons together, it'd be too confusing. Also the jumping caused by the menu being centered is not good (though this is a separate issue and should be fixed regardless). On some tables, including iPad Air in portrait mode, the button also barely fits in.

I'd want to make the button more prominent, both by making it slightly bigger and placing it elsewhere so it's noticeable. Though I'm not sure where myself — perhaps top-right (below the host-supplied top-right UI, which on excalidraw.com is the E+ banner). 
Or maybe bottom left above the help icon. Ideas welcome.
author: dwelle

body:
> * extend this all tools, not just freedraw

I implemented palm rejection only for freedraw based on practical experience. I never had any issues with unwanted strokes with other tools, only freedraw. Also in case of freedraw I disabled pinch zoom, but kept gesture panning. This is also based on experience. Often when I start to draw, my hand touches the screen first causing excalidraw to zoom in or out. This is a problem because the relative size of my drawing, and thus the relative stroke width of the drawing is impacted. Panning however is seldom an issue, and very practical during freedraw.

> * auto-enable this on `pen` event detection (while still allowing to manually disable — although the exact behavior of what should happen in the future afterwards isn't specced out)

Good suggestion. This looks easy to implement.

On this note, I was looking at the touchTimout solution for the context menu, hoping something similar could be developed for pinch zooming… but I couldn’t come up with a solution that worked effectively in practice. This is because the accidental pinch zoom typically happens right at the beginning, when I start to draw, not during I draw… thus enabling pinch zoom based on a touchTimout timer is not a good idea, because I would have pinch zoom enabled just when I start to draw again.

> * reject only `touch` events (allow `mouse` events) — though this is an edge case

I can’t really think of a practical case when this might be an issue. Maybe a laptop with a stylus… though my experience with laptop styluses is overwhelmingly negative. 


placing the lock to the bottom right next to the help sounds good. I also toyed with the idea of having a separate pinch zoom lock next to the zoom, but thought better of it, and discarded the idea.
author: zsviczian

body:
Would you hide the pen lock button in zen mode?
I vote for yes.
author: zsviczian

body:
> I implemented palm rejection only for freedraw based on practical experience. I never had any issues with unwanted strokes with other tools, only freedraw.

I often have issues with at least line tool as well. I'm not sure whether it makes sense to limit this to only a subset of tools. I can imagine people have varying workflows (e.g. lock the tool and then draw many circles/squares while resting the palm).

> Also in case of freedraw I disabled pinch zoom, but kept gesture panning. This is also based on experience. Often when I start to draw, my hand touches the screen first causing excalidraw to zoom in or out. This is a problem because the relative size of my drawing, and thus the relative stroke width of the drawing is impacted. Panning however is seldom an issue, and very practical during freedraw.

I missed this because I didn't think to use 2 fingers to pan (in pen mode). I don't think many people will discover this on their own, but I suppose allowing panning with just one finger would cause issues again.

> On this note, I was looking at the touchTimout solution for the context menu, hoping something similar could be developed for pinch zooming… but I couldn’t come up with a solution that worked effectively in practice. This is because the accidental pinch zoom typically happens right at the beginning, when I start to draw, not during I draw… thus enabling pinch zoom based on a touchTimout timer is not a good idea, because I would have pinch zoom enabled just when I start to draw again.

Unclear — when does accidental pinch zooming happen for you? Unless you are talking when outside the pen mode?

> Would you hide the pen lock button in zen mode?

This is _after_ we auto-enable pen mode? If the auto-enable gets disabled (depends on how design it), then you'd not have any way to enable/disable in zen mode.
author: dwelle

body:
I left the button where it is, but I fixed the jumping tool island. I tried all corners, for me, this is the logical solution still.

I added penDetected to the appState. This is set to true the very first time the user starts to draw with the pen. That is when the pen lock appears.

I renamed penLock to penMode.

penDetected and penMode are reset every time the scene loads. 

penMode now applies equally to all the tools.

I change allowOnPointerDown to your suggestion
```typescript
const allowOnPointerDown =
      !this.state.penMode || event.pointerType !== "touch";
```

The pinch zoom issue happens outside pen mode. Thus I blocked pinch zoom in pen mode.
author: zsviczian

body:
> I left the button where it is, but I fixed the jumping tool island. I tried all corners, for me, this is the logical solution still.

It's logical, but there I still have 2 issues with it.

1. space. On my iPad it's already very crowded, even if we disregard that the top-right is cut off (I'd like to also move the dark mode toggle there later).
	
	Though the penMode button isn't the only issue and a larger redesign is needed.

	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/151715719-ee4ed6a9-17ec-430f-8bad-5549e5aa4dab.png)

2. Even now, people don't know what the tool lock button is doing, and now we're adding another one next to it. Won't help.

But, I'm not gonna block it on this — we can tweak this as we go along.

> I added penDetected to the appState. This is set to true the very first time the user starts to draw with the pen. That is when the pen lock appears.
>
> penDetected and penMode are reset every time the scene loads.

Since it's a session state, can we move it out from appState to the `App` instance (you can pass the `penMode` prop to `LayerUI`)? We can always move it to the appState later when needed, which won't require a migration. In general, the default should be to put into `appState` only that which makes sense to persist (or the host app needs to track, but even then it may make more sense to just expose instead of put into AppState).

---

Progress looking great 👍

body:
> in penMode, touching the canvas (`touch`) when a drawing tool is selected reverts to `selection` tool. Not sure if a recent regr

This feature is there on excalidraw.com as well. I thought it was intentional. All I did was to exclude the freedraw tool from this - again based on my personal experience that during freedraw this switch to selection tool happened frequently as I rest my hand on the screen while drawing.
author: zsviczian

body:
> This feature is there on excalidraw.com as well. I thought it was intentional.

No, what's happening here is we're not fully preventing `touch` events when penMode is enabled. IOW, palm prevention isn't working for tools other than `freedraw`. The reason why it works for `freedraw` right now is because we implicitly tool-lock when drawing with `freedraw`, whereas we don't for other tools.

So what we want to do is simply prevent `touch` events when penMode is on (and we're not using the `selection` tool).

The only question is whether the solution I proposed (return early) is the correct way to do it, but I think it is. I'll push the change so I can test it out on my tablet (I'm too lazy to set up local debugging session).

This is a good improvement. I tested it on iPad with pencil and touch and on Samsung Note with touch and S-Pen. Works as expected.

Not related to this change... but do you have any idea why the context menu won't on a long press with the apple pencil, but does show with a long press with the S-Pen. This difference is the same for both the current excalidraw.com version and this PR.
author: zsviczian

body:
> Not related to this change... but do you have any idea why the context menu won't on a long press with the apple pencil, but does show with a long press with the S-Pen. This difference is the same for both the current excalidraw.com version and this PR.

Good catch. AFAIK iOS doesn't support touch-hold to open contextMenu, so we're detecting it manually. We should include pens in that. ~~It also means this PR doesn't handle iOS touch — gonna fix.~~ never mind, the check is downstream so it applies.
author: dwelle

body:
> What is the purpose of this?
> 
> ```ts
> event.nativeEvent.pointerType === "pen" &&
>           event.button !== POINTER_BUTTON.SECONDARY
> ```

Allow opening contextmenu via the pen button that usually is used for opening contextmenus (it usually emits the same `event.button` type as right-click on a mouse).

I'll add a doc.

body:
I've rewritten the `onLinkOpen` flow to use the standard behavior of doing `event.preventDefault()` if you wish to handle the redirecting yourself. This has two benefits: 1) clarity 2) you don't have to reimplement excalidraw's behavior for external redirects.

We've also stopped passing `link` since it can be retrieved from `element.link` /cc @ad1992

body:
Cool. One thing: we should disable click-through if you drag between `pointerdown` and `pointerup`, if the drag distance is above some threshold (e.g. the `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD` we're using). Otherwise it will open links if you start dragging and release over a link. Can happen often when the links are on elements, not text.

![excal-view-mode-link-drag-bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/152766511-faf3c9be-7b45-4f0f-b32b-5afcb41c5ff8.gif)

(This affects edit mode as well, but to a much smaller degree since the clickable hitboxes are only the small icons.)
author: dwelle

body:
> DRAGGING_THRESHOLD

I am instead checking the pointer down and pointer up coords are exactly same as for click event it should be same then only redirect
author: ad1992

exact match is too strict. I tend to make micro movements a lot when clicking using a mouse.
author: dwelle

body:
> > I am instead checking the pointer down and pointer up coords are exactly same as for click event it should be same then only redirect
> 
> exact match is too strict. I tend to make micro movements a lot when clicking using a mouse.

But when its a click the pointer events will be same else it means that there was some movement?
author: ad1992

body:
> But when its a click the pointer events will be same else it means that there was some movement?

There is a tiny movement, yes. Hence the `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD` (10px), which we consider as unintentional.

Hello,
Small question about this PR - what is the reason behind commit [b21abd5](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4735/commits/b21abd51a77e4fb091bd788a786fd0e2c0031af4) - disable the feature on mobile? author: yeger00

body:
> Hello, Small question about this PR - what is the reason behind commit [b21abd5](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4735/commits/b21abd51a77e4fb091bd788a786fd0e2c0031af4) - disable the feature on mobile? It might make a difference for an app I'm working on, and I disabled it on a local branch and it seems to be working as expected.
> 
> Thanks, Avi

@yeger00 the only reason for not making the element clickable in mobile was since there isn't any tooltip when you hover over the item so it might not be very intutive that it would open a link as soon as you click on it.
Would like to know your reasoning behind enabling it on mobile and based on that we can surely revisit this :)
author: ad1992

body:
@ad1992  Thank you for your comment.
This is still a WIP, but I'm using Excalidraw as the platform to easily create "roadmap" for training. As an example, I'm working on some general "web development" roadmap [here](https://yeger00.github.io/excalidraw-app/) (and the code is [here](https://github.com/yeger00/excalidraw-app)), but I plan to use it a training platform for newcomers in my work as well.
In this example, I patched both the "click" in mobile, and removed the blue icon for links (as suggested [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6370)).

Let me know what you think.
Thanks,
Avi

body:
@dwelle, @ad1992: I think this is now ready. 

I simply removed the strict pointerDown/Up check because 
1. I see no negative effects of checking only for DRAGGING_THRESHOLD, and 
2. to avoid the issue with a precise check for all touchscreens, not just iPads. I tested the solution with my Lenovo ThinkPad Windows laptop and my Chromebook Due tablet as well. Both have touch screens. The links did not work with the strict check on either of them, and they work great with this PR.

I removed the check for isMobile from `handleCanvasPointerUp` for the same reason.
author: zsviczian

> 
> 1. I see no negative effects of checking only for DRAGGING_THRESHOLD, and

 We want to avoid using `DRAGGING_THRESHOLD` unless its view mode since it becomes annoying during view mode in desktop when link opens but the intention is to move the element hence in this case strict check is preferred
 
![Untitled_ Feb 9, 2022 11_48 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/153133634-58903528-ea9f-4a61-bc63-288dd6ecee74.gif)

> 2. to avoid the issue with a precise check for all touchscreens, not just iPads. I tested the solution with my Lenovo ThinkPad Windows laptop and my Chromebook Due tablet as well. Both have touch screens. The links did not work with the strict check on either of them, and they work great with this PR.
> 
> I removed the check for isMobile from `handleCanvasPointerUp` for the same reason.

It would be better to skip the computing if `hitlinkElement` already exists which would be present in case of desktop

body:
Hi @ad1992, @dwelle,
I wanted to bring this PR to your attention once more. This is an easy fix for tablets, where, if you start to create a multielement with two fingers, there is no way to stop editing because the finalize button is not displayed. The finalize button is only displayed when isMobile is true.
If you'd want to hide the finalize button for non-touch screens, we could implement the isTouchScreen approach here as well. However, I don't see the negative effect of displaying the finalize check button on non-touchscreens.
author: zsviczian

body: Sure lets show this for `isTouchScreen`, for desktop devices its not needed so lets not add it and keep the UI minimal
author: ad1992

body:
@zsviczian sorry for missing this. I somewhat agree with @ad1992 — while I think we could leave it for desktop just fine (it's contextual), the UX isn't the best when using a mouse or any pointing device which actually moves the cursor across canvas, because it will preview the last point all the way until you click the button.

That said, maybe the bad UX is worth the added clarity for those who would otherwise be stuck in the creation mode (i.e. those who don't read tooltips or cannot use keyboard for some reason. Also those whom we incorrectly detect as `!isTouchScreen`)
author: dwelle

body:
> @zsviczian sorry for missing this. I somewhat agree with @ad1992 — while I think we could leave it for desktop just fine (it's contextual), the UX isn't the best when using a mouse or any pointing device which actually moves the cursor across canvas, because it will preview the last point all the way until you click the button.
> 
> That said, maybe the bad UX is worth the added clarity for those who would otherwise be stuck in the creation mode (i.e. those who don't read tooltips or cannot use keyboard for some reason. Also those whom we incorrectly detect as `!isTouchScreen`)

As a user I just click on the same point to stop editing. As per the tooltips, if people are using multi point editing, there is a high chance they would have read the tooltip to get started otherwise its a tendency to draw a line when not reading tooltip. Also we shouldn't add another action coz we are assuming that user might miss tooltips and instead make the tooltip better, and the space could be utilised for hidden actions which are available only via keyboard eg "zoom to fit" so for desktop users I still think we should avoid showing it unless there is an explicit need
author: ad1992

So I am one of those users who missed reading the tooltip 😳 and struggled to finalize the line I started with two fingers on my iPad... Why do we have the finalize button on the mobile toolbar? BTW I missed that as well, until @dwelle brought it to my attention. In short the learning might be that I should pay more attention to tooltips, but also that this function is somehow not intuitive (at least not for me)... I will make the change to only show the finalize button if the user initiated the action with a touch.

body:
> > As a user I just click on the same point to stop editing. As per the tooltips, if people are using multi point editing, there is a high chance they would have read the tooltip to get started otherwise its a tendency to draw a line when not reading tooltip. Also we shouldn't add another action coz we are assuming that user might miss tooltips and instead make the tooltip better, and the space could be utilised for hidden actions which are available only via keyboard eg "zoom to fit" so for desktop users I still think we should avoid showing it unless there is an explicit need
> 
> So I am one of those users who missed reading the tooltip 😳 and struggled to finalize the line I started with two fingers on my iPad... Why do we have the finalize button on the mobile toolbar? BTW I missed that as well, until @dwelle brought it to my attention. In short the learning might be that I should pay more attention to tooltips, but also that this function is somehow not intuitive (at least not for me)... I will make the change to only show the finalize button if the user initiated the action with a touch.

Yes definitely the tooltips are not great right now and could be easily missed thats why we should make the tooltips better for sure and we will soon as well :). What I meant is on Desktop there is a very less chance that user would start multi point without looking at tooltip as the general tendency is to draw a line otherwise I feel. And I do agree the finalise button is not a great UX as well so once we redesign the editor this will be taken care as well.
author: ad1992

body:
The double-tap to finalize isn't a good UX on mobile/touch, so we'll want the finalize button no matter if people read tooltips or not.

Though I must say the current positioning of the finalize button next to the undo buttons will most likely be missed by users too.
author: dwelle

body:
> The double-tap to finalize isn't a good UX on mobile/touch, so we'll want the finalize button no matter if people read tooltips or not.
> 
> Though I must say the current positioning of the finalize button next to the undo buttons will most likely be missed by users too.

Yes thats why I said lets have the finalise button (current one) for mobile/touch devices only as I don't see the need for desktop devices, later on when we redesign we can have it for all devices if needed

body:
So this is my proposed solution.

The finalize button is only displayed if the user is working on a touchscreen device (and only after the first touch event)...

I replaced IsMobileContext with DeviceInfoContext. DeviceInfo has currently two values: isMobile and isTouchScreen. In a next PR I could also add penDetected to DeviceInfo, thus moving penDetected out of AppState.

I learned something today.
Please review and let me know if you are now ok to merge this into master. Once this is merged, as a next PR, I will add penMode to device type.

body:
Note that the link behavior around non-textual elements isn't ideal as you need to click on the paths themselves, not the area:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/155711936-eb6ab34d-6d66-4a42-964d-2f4ba9663ab3.png)

But we can improve later if needed.
author: dwelle

body:
Yeah, it's not ideal, I think we could get away with making a rectangle around the element and adding a link to that (that seems to be the pervasive wisdom on stackoverflow on how to handle this). 

I think at that point, people should just make those elements themselves (for your example, add a rectangle behind the one you made) - we could do it for them, but this could end up being frustrating or inconsistent (especially with hand-drawn shapes or circles).

body:
I'd rename `verticalAlign` to `textVerticalAlign`, since the former is too generic.

A few bugs:

1. the text align icons don't have active state when wysiwyg active
2. the text align is reset to `top` after you edit
3. bottom vertical align acts incorrectly in specific cases, such as this:
	  1. create a sticky note of single line of text, and exit wysiwyg
	  2. set vertical align to bottom, and horizontal align to left or right
	  3. edit again, and add multiple new lines
	  
	  ![vertical_align_bottom_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/156341568-509c5cd6-fb3a-4527-890a-c252d697b10a.gif)
author: dwelle
